premises-NL: All students in the dual degree program must maintain a GPA of 3.5 or higher.
If a student completes a thesis, then they qualify for the second degree.
If a student does not participate in leadership activities, then they are not eligible for international exchange.
All students are required to complete a capstone project.
All students are eligible for international exchange.
If a student completes the capstone project, then they complete a thesis.
If a student participates in leadership activities, then they are in the dual degree program.
All students are in the dual degree program.
logic_output_raw:
{
  "facts": [],
  "rules": [
    "ForAll(x, InDualDegreeProgram(x) -> GPA(x) >= 3.5)",
    "ForAll(x, CompletesThesis(x) -> QualifiesForSecondDegree(x))",
    "ForAll(x, NOT ParticipatesInLeadershipActivities(x) -> NOT EligibleForInternationalExchange(x))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> CompletesCapstoneProject(x))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> EligibleForInternationalExchange(x))",
    "ForAll(x, CompletesCapstoneProject(x) -> CompletesThesis(x))",
    "ForAll(x, ParticipatesInLeadershipActivities(x) -> InDualDegreeProgram(x))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> InDualDegreeProgram(x))"
  ],
  "existentials": []
}

premises-NL: All students pay the required fees within the specified period.
If x does not pay the required fees, then x is not allowed to continue studies at the university.
If x is not allowed to continue studying, then x has not paid the fees.
All students submitted their registration request.
If a student did not complete prerequisite courses, then they cannot register for advanced subjects.
If a student missed the deadline, then they cannot register for any courses.
All students completed the prerequisite verification.
All students added at least one course.
All students successfully registered for courses.
If a student completed the prerequisite check, then they could enroll in waitlisted courses.
logic_output_raw:
{
  "facts": [],
  "rules": [
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> SubmittedRegistrationRequest(x))",
    "ForAll(x, (NOT CompletedPrerequisiteCourses(x) -> CannotRegisterForAdvancedSubjects(x)))",
    "ForAll(x, MissedDeadline(x) -> CannotRegisterForAnyCourses(x))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> CompletedPrerequisiteVerification(x))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> Exists(y, Course(y) AND AddedCourse(x, y)))",
    "ForAll(x, Student(x) -> SuccessfullyRegisteredForCourses(x))",
    "ForAll(x, CompletedPrerequisiteCheck(x) -> Exists(y, Course(y) AND EnrollInWaitlistedCourses(x, y)))"
  ],
  "existentials": []
}

rules: ForAll(x, Student(x) -> Exists(y, Course(y) AND AddedCourse(x, y)))
ForAll(x, CompletedPrerequisiteCheck(x) -> Exists(y, Course(y) AND EnrollInWaitlistedCourses(x, y)))

If a student lacks qualifications, then they lack proficiency.

If there exists at least one student with understanding, then there exists at least one student with training.

If a student lacks reasoning skills, then they lack training.
If a student has proficiency, then they have understanding.

The ontology has the predicate "HasUnderstanding" which takes a Student. So the rule would be ForAll(x, Student(x) -> HasUnderstanding(x)).

Next premise: "There exists at least one student with training." This is an existential statement. The ontology has "HasTraining" as a predicate for Student. So the existential would be Exists(x, Student(x) AND HasTraining(x)).

Third premise: "If a student has understanding, then they have reasoning skills." This is a conditional statement, so a rule. Using the predicates "HasUnderstanding" and "HasReasoningSkills", the rule would be ForAll(x, (HasUnderstanding(x) -> HasReasoningSkills(x))).

Fourth premise: "All students have skills." Another universal statement. The predicate here is "HasSkills". So ForAll(x, Student(x) -> HasSkills(x)).

Fifth premise: "There exists at least one student with understanding." This is an existential. Using "HasUnderstanding", it's Exists(x, Student(x) AND HasUnderstanding(x)).

Sixth premise: "If a student lacks skills, then they lack proficiency." This is a conditional. The ontology has "LacksSkills" and "LacksProficiency". So ForAll(x, (LacksSkills(x) -> LacksProficiency(x))).
